_readme: Human-labelled gold subset for validating the advisory LLM-judge (DR-0018). Self-contained and INDEPENDENT of the generated eval corpus: each case pairs a hand-authored scenario with a diagnosis and the verdict a human reviewer assigns on the rubric (correct_cause = does the top hypothesis identify the gold root cause; evidence_valid = is the cited evidence real and relevant; verdict = correct_cause AND evidence_valid, matching RubricVerdict.passed). evals/validate_judge.py runs the LLM-judge on these and reports judge-vs-human agreement. Labels are the maintainer's ground truth, reviewed at merge.

logs.msg: TypeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'amount' in payments.charge
TypeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'amount' in payments.charge

bad_deploy.gold_cause: Bad deploy d3f00d5 reworked payments.charge and introduced a NoneType error; /pay 500s begin ~30s later.

config_error.gold_cause: Config change c0ffee1 removed the SMTP_URL default; /notify raises KeyError shortly after.

logs.msg: TimeoutError: could not acquire a connection
TimeoutError: could not acquire a connection

commits.msg: perf: hold db connections open across requests

resource.gold_cause: Deploy beefca7 held db connections open; db_pool_in_use climbed to its ceiling and /report requests time out acquiring a connection.

hypotheses.cause: Deploy d3f00d5 reworked payments.charge and passed a None where an amount was expected, so /pay returns 500s.

cases.rationale: Names the gold cause and cites the first error log + the culprit commit.
Wrong cause (no DB signal) and cites the unrelated docs decoy commit.
Cause is roughly right, but the cited log 0 is a healthy INFO line, not evidence of the failure.
Abstaining is wrong when the gold has a clear cause backed by an error log + the culprit deploy.

hypotheses.cause: Commit c0ffee1 removed the SMTP_URL default, so /notify raises KeyError.

cases.rationale: Names the gold config cause and cites the KeyError log + the config commit.

hypotheses.cause: A code regression in the mailer function crashed /notify.

cases.rationale: Misattributes a config error to a code bug (wrong cause), though the cited KeyError log is real and relevant.

hypotheses.cause: A config change removed the SMTP_URL default.

cases.rationale: Right cause, but cites the unrelated test decoy commit instead of the config change.

hypotheses.cause: Deploy beefca7 held db connections open; db_pool_in_use hit its ceiling and /report times out acquiring a connection.

cases.rationale: Names the gold cause and cites the climbing metric + the culprit deploy.

hypotheses.cause: A slow SQL query on /report caused the timeouts.

cases.rationale: Blames a slow query rather than the connection leak/deploy (wrong cause), though the timeout log is real.

hypotheses.cause: A CPU meltdown on /report exhausted the workers.

cases.rationale: Wrong cause and cites a metric series (cpu_meltdown) that does not exist.

hypotheses.cause: Deploy beefca7 exhausted the db connection pool, so /report requests time out.

cases.rationale: Correct cause backed by the culprit deploy + a real timeout log. It omits the distinctive metric handle -- the deterministic judge enforces exact gold handles, but the rubric judges correctness and evidence validity, both of which hold here.
